source.title: Structural Damage - Crane Lifting Incident at Horn Mountain SPAR

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Mississippi Canyon Block 127

actions_taken: Notification to BSEE New Orleans District at 1900 hours
Failed chain jack and nylon slings sent to Broussard Storage Facility for examination
Remaining jacks 8 and 9 sent to Solution Services Houston to repair lifting pad-eyes
New lift plan developed using longer choker slings
Chain jacks 7-8-9 successfully re-installed using revised lift plan
Chain jacks 1-2-3 sent for pad-eye repair
event.summary: On 21 October 2023 at 13:04 hours, platform personnel at Horn Mountain SPAR attempted to lift an 8,300-pound hydraulic chain jack cylinder using the West Crane. When the lift reached 15 feet above deck, two nylon choker slings failed due to contact with sharp edges on the cylinder, causing the load to fall onto two cargo baskets. The lifting authorities had decided not to use the manufacturer-recommended lifting pad-eyes, believing them to be frozen after 22 years in service. Instead, they used nylon choker slings wrapped around the cylinder body. The shorter slings used for this lift were positioned where they contacted sharp edges at the top of the jack, causing them to cut and fail. No personnel injuries occurred, and damage was limited to two cargo baskets. The incident could have been prevented by using the dedicated lifting pad-eyes as recommended by the manufacturer.
recommendations: Recommend OII draft safety alert to inform operators to utilize proper lifting techniques as recommended by the manufacturer
key_phrases: sharp edge near the top of the jack

threats.name: Rigging equipment failure due to sharp edge contact
Use of non-manufacturer recommended lifting method
Inadequate lifting equipment maintenance (pad-eyes not refurbished)

controls.name: Manufacturer lifting pad-eyes
controls.barrier_role: Engineered lifting points to prevent rigging contact with sharp edges
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineered control designed into equipment

supporting_text: The LA's made the decision not to use the dedicated lifting pad-eyes on each jack
The LA's stated that they did not believe the pad-eyes were fully functional, as they had not been used in many years, and were believed to be frozen up
The manufacturer of the hydraulic chain jacks recommends the use of the lifting pad-eyes when lifting, removing, and installing the jacks

controls.name: Lifting pad-eye refurbishment during maintenance
controls.barrier_role: Ensure lifting equipment is maintained in functional condition

supporting_text: when the hydraulic chain jacks were originally sent in to BSF for maintenance, the decision was made not to refurbish the lifting pad-eyes prior to returning offshore
The pad-eyes allow for the jacks to be hoisted horizontally and then pivot vertically as needed
Equipment Failure: Inadequate equipment repair

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for lifting operations
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and control measures for lifting operations
controls.lod_basis: Verification and oversight of work planning

supporting_text: The JSA did not identify the sharp edge near the top of the jack as a hazard for the lifting operation
Management Systems: Inadequate hazard analysis
The LA's developed a Generic Lift Plan, Non-Routine Lifts, and Blind Lifts Job Safety Analysis (JSA)

controls.name: Lifting Authority review and approval
controls.barrier_role: Competent person review of lifting plan and rigging configuration

supporting_text: Anadarko designated two personnel as Lifting Authorities (LA's) to devise a lifting plan
The LA's made the decision not to use the dedicated lifting pad-eyes on each jack
the LA's decided to utilize nylon choker slings attached around the cylinder body to perform the lift
Human Performance Error: Not following proper recommended procedure

controls.name: Properly rated rigging equipment
controls.barrier_role: Ensure rigging has adequate capacity for the load

supporting_text: The slings were properly rated to handle the weight of the load, however due to the poor positioning of the slings around the sharp edges of the cylinder, the sling failed
two 10-foot endless chokers, both rated for 10,600 pounds, which was attached to a 60-foot 4-ply nylon choker rated for 16,320 pounds

The slings used when the chain jack fell were smaller and shorter. Because of this, the slings were placed in a position that would compromise them by being exposed to a sharp edge near the top of the jack

controls.name: Exclusion zone during lifting operations
controls.barrier_role: Prevent personnel injury from dropped objects

supporting_text: There were no personnel near the area where/when the load fell
Prior to the lift being hoisted, personnel had moved away from the lift so as to not be impacted by possible dropped objects
There were no injuries to personnel

controls.name: Crane pre-use inspection
controls.barrier_role: Verify crane equipment is in safe operating condition

supporting_text: The BSEE AI requested and received the following documents: Horn Mountain SPAR Operations Manual, JSA, Personnel on Board (POB), Crane Pre-use, Sling certifications, Crane operator's license/certifications

controls.name: Certified crane operator
controls.barrier_role: Ensure competent operation of lifting equipment

supporting_text: Crane operator's license/certifications

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
